title: baeckea oxycoccoides
intro: Baeckea oxycoccoides, also known as the woolly baeckea, is a shrub native to eastern Australia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and woolly leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Baeckea oxycoccoides include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate coughs and colds, and soothe skin irritations. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in herbal creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
